name: SK hynix Inc.
window: Q1 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 23 → Jul 16, 2026 (Nasdaq ADR debut Jul 10; 2Q full print upcoming)

events.text: Jefferies (Jeff Kim/KBI) argues Korea could out-earn Nvidia in 2027: HBM4 ~35% more expensive than HBM3E and Hynix HBM margins flat (not -20% as feared), with NVIDIA/hyperscalers absorbing the increase. The rally so far has been ASP-led and now needs to turn into unit growth in 2027.

The TSMC ecosystem supplies the logic base die beneath SK Hynix's HBM (Samsung joining the alliance); the expert described Jensen Huang as the supply chain's 'firefighter,' personally chasing Hynix capacity, corroborating Hynix as the primary HBM source on Rubin.

MS: news that Hynix is cutting HBM4 volume/share 'doesn't matter for EPS' — it is better not to have HBM because commodity DRAM returns are higher; but HBM matters for the multiple (DRAM ~4-5x peak earnings vs HBM ~10x). Lower HBM share is EPS-neutral-to-positive but multiple-negative. NAND seen as a more attractive industry than DRAM.

Q1 2026: revenue KRW 52.6tn (+60% QoQ, +198% YoY), operating profit KRW 37.6tn (≈doubled QoQ), OP margin 72% (+13pp QoQ), net income KRW 40.3tn. Net cash KRW 35tn. AI demand drove 'significant price increases in DRAM and NAND'; HBM4 'far exceeding supply capacity.' NAND -10% QoQ off a high base.

JPM: the thesis that Samsung should 'catch up' to Hynix post-strike 'did not work'; Hynix's outperformance is tied to single-stock ETFs + Hynix-centric passive indexing, plus the confirmed US listing/ADR catalyst and HBM4 progress; retail is positioned in Hynix.

GS: cycle longevity is underestimated — Hynix at 5-6x earnings means the market disbelieves the durability; GS's 9x target is 'conservative.' Even a bear case of DRAM -30%/yr in 2027-28 leaves DRAM GM ~40%, supporting the earnings multiple.
Ex-VP of procurement at Micron: HBM becomes a commodity once 2-3 suppliers are qualified; 'your $900 stock goes back to $50' once capacity normalizes — a risk within CY26/early CY27.

JEF calls SK Hynix's ~20% pullback a buying opportunity; the KBI note argues Korean memory can out-earn the hyperscalers. DRAM ASP +50-60% QoQ in 2Q26, +20% QoQ in 3Q26; NAND +75-100% QoQ in 2Q. @rwang07: 'SK Hynix is the next $3 trillion company.'

SK Hynix exits 2026 as #2 in DRAM at ~610K wafers/month (vs Samsung ~740K) but reaches 710K by end-2027, narrowing the gap. It remains dominant in HBM with the largest capacity (~200K end-2026 vs Samsung 180K, Micron 100K). Yongin FAB1 ready end-2027 but output only in 2028; FAB2 just announced. Risk: Samsung could overtake as #1 in HBM in 2027. Anthropic pursuing a deal (DRAM/eSSD 2027, HBM 2028).
HBM supply for 2027 revised down (60→50bn Gb-eq) as conventional DRAM becomes more profitable; LTAs viewed as call options; wafer shortage.

Korea AI mega-plan revised up to ~US$3.1tn (from the $880B/$1.3T reported 06-29): Samsung + SK Group KRW4,755tn through 2040 for semis/AI-DC/robotics; implies doubling installed Korean DRAM capacity within 5 years and pulling the Yongin ramp forward 7-12 years. SK Hynix nearly doubled its AI-DC commitment (+KRW1,000tn) and committed KRW1,100tn to an AI-memory network (MS/Kathleen Oh). Memory execs: supply only ~50% of demand and worsening; US$12-20tn cumulative revenue opportunity.

ADR debut: priced $149, raised ~US$26.5bn — the largest-ever first-time share sale by a foreign company on US markets (past Alibaba). Book 7x oversubscribed, 500+ firms; ADR priced ~3% premium to local (below the ~5-10% MS range); Korean line still ~4.5x fwd P/E. Proceeds earmarked for Korean capacity + more ASML EUV — 'not financial engineering, a direct bet the AI memory shortage is far from over.'

CEO Kwak (NY interviews): 'next year is expected to be the worst year in the industry's history from a supply perspective' — despite aggressive capacity expansion, memory demand will continue to exceed production capacity over the next decade (shortage past 2030); customers signing LTAs in anticipation of a prolonged shortage. Frames the ~US$26.5bn ADR raise as funding tighter alignment with US AI customers.

2Q26 OP preview W60.4tn (+61% QoQ, +556% YoY) — ~8% BELOW the W65tn consensus, the miss driven by SK Hynix's higher HBM revenue mix (HBM ASP hike slower than commodity DRAM) + lower commodity-DRAM px hike + early LTA effect. Cuts 2Q blended-DRAM ASP to +28.9% QoQ (from +50%), reverting to ~+10% market rate in 3Q as HBM4 MP begins. Framed as NOT a downturn — a more realistic reflection of 3-5yr LTAs that smooth earnings (record ~74.6% 2Q OPM, still rising). KIS makes Samsung its top pick over Hynix on LTA-drag.

events.src: Bloomberg / Citi 'Dr Mo' / Goldman PSI (Korea selloff)
events.text: 'SK Hynix Shares Plunge Most on Record in Deepening Korea Selloff' — biggest Seoul drop on record, days after the Nasdaq debut, on the KIS/local 2Q OP-preview miss. Citi (Apabhai): SEC -9.9%, ~$878m of leveraged-ETF hedging sales; Goldman PSI: SEC foreign ownership 46.6% (2009 low), 13-day foreign KOSPI sell streak, memory 'the most crowded trade globally'. A sentiment/technical washout, not a fundamentals downgrade.

Sharp rebound next session — SK Hynix +27% on the day, approaching all-time highs as the US ADR premium kicks in. @JulianKlymochko: '$SKHY is a $1 trillion company trading like a meme stonk' (3.8x next-yr earnings, +493% 1yr). @jimcramer: 'How did all of tech become hostage to SK Hynix!' — leverage to the 'ridiculous leveraged ETFs'. Positioning-driven volatility, not a fundamental change.

On CNBC: 2026 HBM demand strong enough to keep pulling wafer capacity from commodity DRAM; memory undersupplied through 2027, likely into 2028. Nuance on the share-loss bear: in a fully-sold-out HBM market, Samsung gaining HBM share need not come at SK Hynix's expense — the real bottleneck is wafer capacity, not demand. CXMT ~10% of DRAM bits (entrenched) but ~3yrs behind on process, ~3-4yrs behind on HBM.
